title: New Sprite Injecction
reason: What if the shell we open overrides all the agents 'open' commands
requirements: Is it possible to, when the shell starts, override clauge, gemini, and agent, to proxy the full call with the passed in needed vars?
This would enable the user to open and close an agent whenever they want in the agent terminal, and always get whatever specific instructions we want.
We could then even, on terminal opening, create shell level variables that the embedded prompt could just point to. Right now that's just the sprites, but could expand.

This would make the injected prompt always static, and the variables would be terminal level.  they would live through opening and closing any agent in a single session. but not bleed to other sessions.

This could even be a file that gets written, based on the session / session ID.  and then we point it to a file.  Instructions could be written into there even, and the prompt just points to file with 'do this', and then we could use this in future for better sessions persistence.
